info.title: Publishing API

tags.name: Jobs
tags.description: The jobs endpoint is used to manage publishing jobs of the context tenant.

get.description:
Retrieves a specific publishing job with the specified ID, for example, `job_41B25`.
{% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To retrieve a job, your token must include one of the following scopes ` xmcpub.jobs.a:r`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:r`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:rl`. {% /admonition %}

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the job to retrieve.

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
  - Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing)
  - Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token

404.description: Job not found with the specified job ID

get.description:
Lists all publishing jobs for a specific tenant, including jobs that are `in progress`, `completed`, `failed`, and so on. This endpoint supports two types of pagination: - Offset pagination - Checkpoint pagination When you use offset pagination, only the first 1000 records are returned.
{% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To retrieve a list of jobs, your token must include one of the following scopes ` xmcpub.jobs.a:r`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:r`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:rl`. {% /admonition %}

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: The page number of the results to return. The first page is `1`. This parameter must be used only in offset pagination requests as it indicates that offset pagination should be used.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of results per page.

parameters.name: x-sc-continuation
parameters.description: Continuation token value from which to start the selection (checkpoint pagination). The first time you call this endpoint using checkpoint pagination, omit this header.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source value to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values  (for example, `?source=Pages|Sites`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.createdBy.id
parameters.description: The ID of the creator to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values  (for example, `?system.createdBy.id=Qbu3QmWtnQoU0my5jX9MFxBYIeQrFhd2|auth0|000b2fc5fa6b9e5883b6245b`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.createdBy.name
parameters.description: The name of the creator to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values  (for example, `?system.createdBy.name=John+Doe|John+Smith`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.status
parameters.description: The status value to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values (for example, `?system.status=Queued|Running`).

items.description: The status of a publish job.

parameters.name: system.queuedTime
parameters.description: The job queued time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.queuedTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.queuedTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`).  Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

parameters.name: system.startTime
parameters.description: The job start time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.startTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.startTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`). Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

parameters.name: system.finishTime
parameters.description: The job finish time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.finishTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.finishTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`). Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Field to sort jobs by. You can specify whether to sort by ascending or descending order, using the format `field:order`, where order can be `1` for ascending or `-1` for descending (for example, `name:1`). The following fields are supported: `name`, `source`, `system.status`, `system.queuedTime`, `system.startTime`, `system.finishTime`.

400.description: Invalid parameters in request query string

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
  - Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing)
  - Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token

post.description: Creates a new publishing job. {% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To create a publishing job, your token must include one of the following scopes ` xmcpub.jobs.a:w`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:w`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:wl`. Users with a scope of `xmcpub.jobs.t:wl`  (limited write) must also have `Sitecore Client Advanced Publishing` or `Sitecore Client Publishing`  roles or have the `admin` flag set in XM Cloud.  {% /admonition %} {% admonition type="info" name="Note" %} Client token of applications must have the `xmcloud.cm:admin` scope. {% /admonition %}

requestBody.description: The request for creating the job.

400.description:
Bad Request. Possible reasons:
- Invalid name
- Invalid description
- Invalid items
- Invalid mode
- Invalid locales
- Invalid source
- Invalid tenant

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description: Request does not contain required scopes

get.description:
Lists all permissions assigned to the current user.
{% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To retrieve permissions, the token must be valid, but no scope is required. {% /admonition %}

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
- Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing).
- Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token.

get.summary: Retrieve data for publishing job filters
get.description:
Returns data to populate filters for publishing jobs. Returned data is  dynamic and depends on the filters already selected. This operation finds all publishing jobs that match the selected filters and returns distinct values for the `source`, `system.createdBy`, and `system.status` fields of these jobs.
{% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To retrieve publishing jobs that match the selected filters, your token must include one of the following scopes `xmcpub.jobs.a:r`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:r`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:rl`. {% /admonition %}

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source value to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values (for example, `?source=Pages|Sites`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.createdBy.id
parameters.description: The ID of the creator to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values (for example, `?system.createdBy.id=Qbu3QmWtnQoU0my5jX9MFxBYIeQrFhd2|auth0|000b2fc5fa6b9e5883b6245b`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.createdBy.name
parameters.description: The name of the creator to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values (for example, `?system.createdBy.name=John+Doe|John+Smith`). Wildcards are not supported.

parameters.name: system.status
parameters.description: The status value to use when filtering jobs. Use a pipe to specify multiple values (for example, `?system.status=Queued|Running`).

items.description: The status of a publish job.

parameters.name: system.queuedTime
parameters.description: The job queued time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.queuedTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.queuedTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`). Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

parameters.name: system.startTime
parameters.description: The job start time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.startTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.startTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`). Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

parameters.name: system.finishTime
parameters.description: The job finish time to filter entries by. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.finishTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.finishTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`. Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

400.description: Invalid parameters in request query string

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
  - Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing)
  - Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token

get.summary: Retrieve a summary of publishing jobs
get.description:
Retrieves statistics about publishing jobs, including how many jobs are queued, how many are running or have failed or been canceled, and how many have been completed.
{% admonition type="info" name="Note" %}To retrieve job statistics, your token must include one of the following scopes `xmcpub.jobs.a:r`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:r`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:rl`. {% /admonition %}

parameters.name: system.queuedTime
parameters.description: Queued time to use to filter entries. Use `&` to include more than one filter (for example, `?system.queuedTime=>2024-10-14T00:00:00.000Z&system.queuedTime=<2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z`). Only `=>DATE` and `=<DATE` filters are supported.

400.description: Invalid parameters in request query string

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
  - Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing)
  - Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token

post.description:
Cancels a publishing job with a specific ID.


**Note**
To cancel a publishing job, your token must include one of the following
scopes: `xmcpub.jobs.a:w`, `xmcpub.jobs.t:w`, or `xmcpub.jobs.t:wl`.
Users with a scope of `xmcpub.jobs.t:wl` (limited write) must also have a
`Sitecore Client Advanced Publishing` or `Sitecore Client Publishing`
roles or have the `admin` flag set in the XM Cloud instance.
If access token includes only `xmcpub.jobs.t:wl` scope, callers
must be able to cancel only those publishing job which they started.

**Note**
Client tokens of applications must
have the `xmcloud.cm:admin` scope.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: The ID of the publishing job to cancel, for example `job_AE86B`.

401.description: Unauthorized request or invalid access token

403.description:
Forbidden. Possible reasons:
- Authorization failed (required scopes and/or roles are missing).
- Context tenant cannot be resolved from the access token.

404.description: Job not found with the specified job ID

409.description: Job does not have a `Queued` or `Running` status

501.description: Job cancellation is not supported for XM Cloud Content

next.description: Continuation token for the next page.

data.description: The collection of data for response.

source.description: Source of the job. This is typically the application name specified as the source (for example, `Pages`, `Sites`, and so on).

CreateJobRequest.description: The request for creating a job.

CreatedByData.description: Represents the data for the user who created the job.

id.description: ID of the item to publish.

type.description: Type of the item to publish (for example, `Page`, `Site`, `Item`, and so on).

locale.description:
Locale of the item to publish. Items can have a locale equal to `*`, which means that items must be published in all languages. `locale` must be specified for each item or as a list in `options.xmc.locales`.
**Note** A combination of locale in item and in `options.xmc.locales` is not allowed.
Valid format is `xx` or `xx-YY`, where `xx` is ISO-639-1 language code and `YY` is ISO 3166-1 country code.

mode.description: Mode of the items publish job.

publishChildren.description: Whether to publish children items recursively.

Items.description: Represents XMC publishing items options model.

publishChildren.description: Whether to publish children items recursively.

mode.description: Mode of the items publish job.

ItemsOptionsModel.description: Represents the items options for publishing.

lastReportTime.description: The last time the statistics were updated.

data.description: The collection of data for response.

sources.description: The unique list of available sources.

createdBy.description: The unique list of jobs creators.

PublishJobFiltersResponse.description: The response for publishing job filters.

PublishJobResponse.description: The response for creating a job.

PublishJobsSummaryResponse.description: The response for publishing job summary.

items.description: Root items to start publishing from.

canViewDetails.description: Whether details of the publishing job can be viewed.

canCancel.description: Whether the publishing job can be canceled.

PublishingJobPermissions.description: The permissions of the current user or application to work with the job.

ResponsePublishOptionsModel.description: The options the publishing job was created with.

mode.description: Mode of the site publish job.
Mode of the items publish job.

queued.description: The total number of jobs queued.

running.description: The total number of jobs running.

canceled.description: The total number of jobs canceled.

failed.description: The total number of failed jobs.

canceling.description: The number of jobs currently being canceled.

StatisticsCounts.description: The job count for every status.

organizationId.description: The ID of the organization that the job belongs to.

tenantId.description: The ID of the tenant that the job belongs to.

tenantJobId.description: The job ID on the tenant side.

queuedTime.description: The time when the job was queued.

startTime.description: The time when the job was started.

finishTime.description: The time when the job was finished.

SystemData.description: The system data of a job.

canReadAll.description: Whether the user can read all jobs.

canReadOwn.description: Whether the user can read their own jobs.

canViewDetailsOfAll.description: Whether the user can view details of all jobs.

canViewDetailsOfOwn.description: Whether the user can view details of their own jobs.

canCreate.description: Whether the user can create jobs.

UserPermissions.description: The permissions assigned to a user.

locales.description:
Locales in which content is to be published. This field is required for XM Cloud site publishing jobs (when `options.xmc.site` is set) and is optional for XM Cloud items publishing jobs.
**Note** When it is specified for items publishing none of the items should have `locale` set.
The format of each element in array must be `xx` or `xx-YY`, where `xx` is ISO-639-1 language code and `YY` is ISO 3166-1 country code.

XmcPublishingModel.description: Represents a model for XMC publishing.

locales.description: Locales in which content is to be published.

additionalItemsSent.description: The number of additional items sent.

itemsSendingComplete.description: Indicates whether the sending of items is complete.

XmcStatistics.description: Additional fields of the XMC statistics.
